refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined productivity target by optimizing equipment operation and task execution efficiency.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and outcome-focused; refining it into sub-FRs clarifies specific functional aspects that contribute to productivity, enabling targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure productivity metrics (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations and verify that sub-functions contribute to meeting or exceeding the target.
sub_frs: FR1: Optimize equipment operational parameters to maximize throughput.
FR2: Efficiently schedule and execute worksite tasks to minimize idle time.
FR3: Provide real-time performance feedback to operators and tele-operators.

candidate_dps: DP1: Adaptive control algorithms for equipment operation optimization.
DP2: Task scheduling and dispatch system integrated with worksite management.
DP3: Real-time telemetry and feedback interface for operator awareness.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Adaptive control algorithms for equipment operation optimization.

potential_sources: Interaction between control algorithms and task scheduling may cause coupling.
Telemetry feedback loops could introduce dependencies.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 is logically separable from task scheduling and telemetry feedback, enabling modular design.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate sensor data for control algorithms.
Risk that adaptive control may not fully capture all operational variabilities.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating task scheduling DP in next decomposition level to manage coupling.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and embedded control systems responsible for equipment operation and control.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends operational data and performance metrics to other modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task execution commands and schedules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused on embedded control specifics.
Highest containment score due to direct control over equipment operation.
Supports remote control but not primary for adaptive control algorithms.
Focuses on safety alerts, less relevant for productivity control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Adaptive control algorithms are best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize interface complexity and maximize control fidelity.
containment_risks: Potential tight coupling with Teleoperation if remote overrides are frequent.
Risk of diffusion if control logic is duplicated in Teleoperation or NGHE Solution modules.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish clear interface contracts with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution to avoid diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (adaptive control algorithms) logically satisfies the refined FR of optimizing equipment operation for productivity.

diffusion_risks: Possible duplication of control logic across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
Interface complexity if Telemetry and Tasking interfaces are not well defined.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment suitability; interfaces are well aligned with known interfaces.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring productivity metrics and control system logs within the Vehicle module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of adaptive control DP to Vehicle IM with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Refine interface definitions between Vehicle and Teleoperation to reduce diffusion risk.
Plan next level decomposition for task scheduling and telemetry feedback functions.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition and assignments are sound, the coupling with task scheduling and telemetry feedback requires further refinement to reduce diffusion risks and improve modular clarity.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Optimize equipment operational parameters to maximize throughput.
FR2: Efficiently schedule and execute worksite tasks to minimize idle time.
FR3: Provide real-time performance feedback to operators and tele-operators.

revise.why: To incorporate task scheduling and telemetry feedback DPs explicitly and address coupling and interface clarity.

assumptions: Sensor data quality is sufficient for adaptive control.
Interfaces Telemetry and Tasking are stable and well-defined.
open_risks: Coupling between control and scheduling functions.
evidence_needed: Operational data demonstrating control algorithm effectiveness.
Interface usage logs between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
